items.headline: Major commercial project underway on West San Carlos Street
items.summary: A $696,570 commercial/industrial development is being constructed at 1300 W San Carlos St, representing significant economic activity in San José's commercial corridor. This is the largest single project in this permit batch.

items.headline: Cupertino launches business license amnesty program for shopping centers
items.summary: The city is offering a business license amnesty program targeting shopping center businesses, likely to bring unlicensed or non-compliant businesses into compliance. This could affect local commerce and tax revenue while helping small business owners avoid penalties.

items.headline: City council approves economic incentives for retail and startups
items.summary: Cupertino approved new incentive programs designed to attract and support retail businesses and startups in the city. The incentives could influence where new businesses locate and expand in the South Bay.

items.headline: Great American Litter Pick-up approved as annual city event
items.summary: The City Council approved the Great American Litter Pick-up as an official annual city-sponsored event, authorizing city funding and donations for materials and services. This recurring event engages residents in cleaning up San José neighborhoods.

items.headline: Community Development Grants Presented to Housing Commission
items.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission reviewed funding applicants for HOME and Community Development Block Grants (CDBG) on March 25. These federal funds support affordable housing and community development projects in Sunnyvale.

items.headline: San Antonio Road Area Plan gets transportation alternatives study
items.summary: The Planning Commission will examine land use and transportation options for the San Antonio Road area. This study could affect how traffic, development, and transit work in that corridor.

items.headline: City updates zoning rules to boost retail business vitality
items.summary: Palo Alto is amending building and zoning codes to implement retail policies from its Comprehensive Plan. The changes aim to support and strengthen retail districts across the city.

items.headline: Campbell updates beekeeping rules with new ordinance
items.summary: The City Council adopted an updated beekeeping ordinance on March 24, which may affect residents who keep bees or live near beekeeping operations. The changes set new standards for urban beekeeping in Campbell.
